methodology: Weighted fixture score = fdr_avg (5-GW window) + 0.3 penalty for away venue on next fixture. Lower score = easier run. Fixture rank 1 = best (easiest).

fixture_ratings.verdict: BEST RUN — joint-easiest home fixture in GW38 plus the softest 5-GW average of all assets. Strong transfer target for attackers.
EXCELLENT — home FDR 2 in GW38, 2.4 average across 5 GWs. Consistently favourable run makes MCI assets prime captaincy and transfer targets.
GOOD — home FDR 2 in GW38, solid 2.5 5-GW average. Budget-friendly differential with a kind run-in.
NEUTRAL — home fixture but FDR 3 in GW38, 2.6 average is reasonable. Fitness doubt (chance_next 75%) is a bigger risk than fixtures.
AVOID — away FDR 3 in GW38 plus a 2.8 5-GW average makes CRY one of the tougher runs for attacking assets.
WORST RUN — away FDR 3 in GW38 and the highest 5-GW average of all six assets. Form and ownership remain high but fixtures are the least supportive of captaincy.

best_def_run: Gabriel (ARS) — rank 4 overall; best defensive asset by fixtures despite the moderate FDR 3 home game. Fitness caveat applies.
best_attack_run: Mbeumo (BRE) — rank 1 overall; home FDR 2 with the softest 5-GW average, ideal for attacking transfers.
summary: GW38 fixture picture is clean — no DGWs or BGWs detected for any of the six tracked assets. Easiest runs belong to BRE (Mbeumo, weighted 2.2) and MCI (Haaland, 2.4), both playing FDR-2 fixtures at home. AVL (Rogers, 2.5) is the standout budget option. LIV (M.Salah) has the hardest run: FDR-3 away plus the highest 5-GW average, making him the weakest captaincy pick on fixtures alone this week.
